mnemonics.title: ACID Properties
mnemonics.content: ACID: A Transaction Must Be 'A C I D'eal - Atomic (all or nothing), Consistent (valid state), Isolated (independent), Durable (permanent)

mnemonics.title: Transaction States Flow
mnemonics.content: Remember flow: ACTIVE → PARTIALLY COMMITTED → COMMITTED. Failed goes to ABORTED, then can RESTART or KILL.

mnemonics.title: Concurrency Problems
mnemonics.content: Lost Update, Dirty Read, Non-Repeatable Read, Phantom Read = LDPN (Lost Dirt, Please Never Read)

mnemonics.title: Two-Phase Locking
mnemonics.content: 2PL: GROW first, then SHRINK. Like a balloon - inflate (grow locks), then let air out (shrink/release). Never reinflate!

acronyms.usage: Four fundamental properties that guarantee reliable transaction processing in databases
Concurrency control protocol with Growing and Shrinking phases for ensuring serializability
Lock type that allows multiple transactions to read a data item simultaneously
Lock type that allows only one transaction to read and write a data item
Operations on data items in a transaction schedule
Person responsible for managing database transactions and recovery

visualTips.visualization: Draw a circle with 5 dots: Active → Partial → Committed (top arc), Partial → Failed (right), Failed → Aborted (bottom), Aborted can loop back to Active (restart) or end. Think of it like a journey: Start (Active) → Complete (Committed) OR Fail → Cleanup (Aborted)

Draw a horizontal timeline with a mountain peak: Rising slope (Growing Phase) where locks accumulate, peak (lock point), then falling slope (Shrinking Phase) where locks are released. Never go uphill again after starting downhill!
Draw a precedence graph with transactions as nodes and conflicts as directed edges. If cycles exist → NOT serializable. Think: 'Cycle = Danger, No Cycle = Safe Passage'
Create a 2x2 grid: S-S = YES (compatible), S-X = NO, X-S = NO, X-X = NO. Only Shared locks play nicely together!

analogies.analogy: Think of a money transfer like cash withdrawal from ATM - either you get money AND your account is debited, OR neither happens. The transaction is all-or-nothing like flipping a coin.
Imagine two people writing on the same paper with different colored pens. Without isolation, they'd scribble over each other's work. With isolation, one finishes completely before the other starts.
Like a saved document in a cloud service - once you click save (commit), even if your computer crashes, the document persists on the server.
Similar to making a hotel reservation: First you book all your rooms (growing phase), THEN you check out and release them (shrinking phase). You can't book new rooms after starting to check out!
Two people at a buffet: Person A holds the pasta dish waiting for the salad, while Person B holds the salad waiting for pasta. Neither can proceed - classic deadlock!
